title: erythroxylum umbrosum
intro: Erythroxylum umbrosum is a plant species belonging to the Erythroxylum genus, native to the tropical regions of South America. It is also known as the "Coca of the Forest" due to its similarity in appearance and medicinal properties to the Erythroxylum coca plant. Erythroxylum umbrosum has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as pain, fever, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed for their medicinal benefits, although its availability and use may be limited due to regulations and lack of scientific research.
